slides.title: Segment Trees
bullets: Presentation roadmap with 5 core sections
Progressive coverage from fundamentals to practical understanding
Focus on concepts, trade-offs, and real-world applicability
slides.speaker_notes: Use this slide to introduce scope and flow.

slides.title: What are Segment Trees?
bullets: A binary indexed tree (BIT) data structure used for storing information about segments or intervals of a data set.
Allows for efficient querying and updating of data within specific ranges.
Commonly used in applications requiring range-based queries, such as data compression, image processing, and scientific simulations.
slides.speaker_notes: Make sure to explain the concept of binary indexed trees and how segment trees build upon this concept.

slides.title: Applications of Segment Trees
bullets: Range sum queries: Find the sum of elements within a specific range.
Range minimum/maximum queries: Find the minimum or maximum element within a specific range.
Range update queries: Update the value of elements within a specific range.
Data compression: Segment trees can be used to compress data by representing ranges of identical values.
slides.speaker_notes: Highlight the importance of segment trees in various fields, such as data analysis and scientific simulations.

slides.title: Segment Trees in Computer Science
bullets: Used in various algorithms, including range-based queries and data compression.
Can be applied to various data structures, such as arrays and linked lists.
Has applications in fields like data analysis, scientific simulations, and image processing.
Segment trees provide an efficient way to store and query data within specific ranges.
slides.speaker_notes: Emphasize the significance of segment trees in computer science and their impact on various fields.

slides.title: What is a Segment Tree?
bullets: A data structure used for storing information about intervals, or segments, of a dataset.
It allows for efficient range queries and updates.
Segment trees are a type of binary indexed tree.
slides.speaker_notes: In this context, an interval is a range of values within the dataset.

slides.title: Components of a Segment Tree
bullets: Each node represents a segment of the dataset.
Each node stores the minimum/maximum value of the segment it represents.
Leaf nodes represent single elements of the dataset.
Internal nodes represent a combination of child segments.
slides.speaker_notes: The choice of minimum or maximum value depends on the specific use case.

slides.title: How a Segment Tree Works
bullets: When a query or update is made, it starts at the root node.
The tree is traversed based on the query or update operation.
Each node's value is combined with its child nodes' values to compute the result.
This process continues until the leaf nodes are reached.
slides.speaker_notes: The specific combination of values depends on the type of query or update.

slides.title: Introduction to Segment Tree Construction
bullets: A segment tree is a binary tree data structure where each node represents a segment of the array.
The segment tree is constructed by recursively dividing the array into smaller segments.
Each node in the segment tree contains the minimum or maximum value of the corresponding segment.
slides.speaker_notes: Review the concept of segment trees and their importance in range queries.

slides.title: Segment Tree Construction Algorithm
bullets: Create a node for the segment tree with the following properties: left child, right child, and value.
The value of the node is the minimum or maximum value of the corresponding segment.
The left child of the node represents the left half of the segment, and the right child represents the right half.
If the segment has only one element, the node's value is the element itself.
slides.speaker_notes: Explain the recursive approach to constructing the segment tree.

slides.title: Example Code: Constructing a Segment Tree
bullets: The following Python code demonstrates the construction of a segment tree from a given array.
It uses a recursive approach to divide the array into smaller segments and create nodes for the segment tree.
slides.speaker_notes: Walk through the code and explain each step of the construction process.

slides.title: Range Queries in Segment Trees
bullets: A range query in a segment tree involves finding the sum of elements in a specific range of the array.
This is achieved by traversing the segment tree from the root node down to the leaf nodes.
The root node represents the entire array, and each child node represents a subset of the array.
slides.speaker_notes: Emphasize the importance of range queries in segment trees.
slides.section: Range Queries and Updates in Segment Trees

slides.title: Range Query Algorithm
bullets: Start at the root node and compare the query range with the node's range.
If the query range overlaps with the node's range, recursively query the left and right child nodes.
If the query range is contained within the node's range, return the node's value.
slides.speaker_notes: Highlight the recursive nature of the range query algorithm.
slides.section: Range Queries and Updates in Segment Trees

slides.title: Updating a Range in a Segment Tree
bullets: To update a range in a segment tree, first update the leaf nodes that correspond to the updated range.
Then, recursively update the parent nodes until the root node is reached.
This ensures that the segment tree remains balanced and efficient for future queries.
slides.speaker_notes: Discuss the importance of maintaining a balanced segment tree.
slides.section: Range Queries and Updates in Segment Trees

slides.title: Example Use Cases of Segment Trees
bullets: Range Sum Query: Find the sum of all elements in a given range in an array.
Range Minimum/Maximum Query: Find the minimum/maximum element in a given range in an array.
Update Range: Update the value of all elements in a given range in an array.
slides.speaker_notes: Segment trees can be used in various applications such as data compression, machine learning, and scientific simulations.
slides.section: Example Use Cases and Code Implementation

slides.title: Range Sum Query with Segment Trees
bullets: Create a segment tree with n nodes, where each node represents an interval [l, r].
For each node, store the sum of all elements in the interval [l, r].
To query the sum of elements in a range [a, b], traverse the segment tree from the root node to the leaf node that covers the range [a, b].
slides.speaker_notes: The time complexity of range sum query using segment trees is O(log n).
slides.section: Example Use Cases and Code Implementation

slides.title: Range Minimum/Maximum Query with Segment Trees
bullets: Create a segment tree with n nodes, where each node represents an interval [l, r].
For each node, store the minimum/maximum element in the interval [l, r].
To query the minimum/maximum element in a range [a, b], traverse the segment tree from the root node to the leaf node that covers the range [a, b].
slides.speaker_notes: The time complexity of range minimum/maximum query using segment trees is O(log n).
slides.section: Example Use Cases and Code Implementation
